agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"API Gateway","to":"OrderService","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID propagated via X-Trace-ID header; structured logs correlated."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"Kafka","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID embedded in Kafka message headers alongside event payload."},{"from":"Kafka","to":"FulfillmentWorker","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker discards the trace ID in Kafka message headers and generates a new one. This severs the distributed trace — every downstream operation from this point is uncorrelatable with the original request."},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"NotificationService","status":"broken","issue":"HTTP call made without any trace ID header. NotificationService starts a fresh trace, making notification failures impossible to correlate to the originating order."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"AuditService","status":"risk","issue":"No trace ID propagated over HTTP. Audit records cannot be correlated to the request that triggered them. Marked risk (not broken) because OrderService at least has the trace ID internally — fix is straightforward."},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"AuditService","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker already lost the original trace ID, and does not propagate even its own new trace ID. AuditService starts yet another isolated trace. Audit records from fulfillment are completely orphaned."}],"contract_compliance":"Trace ID contract is honored only in the synchronous API Gateway → OrderService path and the OrderService → Kafka publish step. The contract is violated at every boundary downstream of Kafka consumption. There is no enforced schema or middleware ensuring trace propagation — it relies on each service manually doing the right thing.","data_flow_integrity":"Data flow is functionally intact (orders are processed, notifications sent, audits written), but the observability data flow is fractured into three or more disconnected trace trees per request. A single user order generates at least 3 unrelated trace IDs: one from the gateway, one from FulfillmentWorker, and one from NotificationService — plus additional orphans in AuditService."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker logs use a locally-generated trace ID that cannot be joined to the upstream request trace","NotificationService logs are completely isolated — no correlation to the order or fulfillment that triggered them","AuditService logs from fulfillment-triggered calls have no linkage to any upstream trace","No evidence of structured error logging standards across services (e.g., consistent error codes, correlation fields)"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Per-service metrics exist (counters, histograms) but no cross-service or end-to-end metrics","gaps":["No end-to-end order latency metric (API Gateway receive → Notification sent)","No cross-service SLO tracking (e.g., p99 order-to-fulfillment time)","No Kafka consumer lag metric mentioned for FulfillmentWorker","No error-rate correlation across services (a spike in NotificationService errors cannot be linked to FulfillmentWorker issues without manual investigation)","No business-level metrics (orders fulfilled per minute, notification delivery rate)"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Broken — effective distributed tracing covers only 2 of 5 services (API Gateway and OrderService). The remaining 3 services operate with orphaned traces.","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker must extract trace ID from Kafka message headers and use it as its active trace context","FulfillmentWorker must propagate trace ID in outbound HTTP calls to NotificationService and AuditService","OrderService must propagate trace ID in HTTP calls to AuditService","Without these fixes, any tracing backend (Jaeger, Zipkin, Datadog) will show fragmented, single-service traces instead of the full request waterfall"]},"debugging_capability":"Severely degraded for any issue involving fulfillment or downstream. An on-call engineer at 3am can trace a request from the gateway through OrderService and into Kafka, but the trail goes cold at FulfillmentWorker. Debugging a failed notification or missing audit record requires manually correlating timestamps across 3+ independent trace IDs — this is error-prone and slow under incident pressure."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"FulfillmentWorker crashes mid-processing after consuming Kafka event","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Ensure Kafka consumer uses manual offset commits only after successful processing. Verify dead-letter queue exists for poison messages. Without trace ID, diagnosing which order was affected requires timestamp-based log correlation."},{"scenario":"NotificationService is down or slow","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"FulfillmentWorker needs a timeout, retry with backoff, and circuit breaker for NotificationService calls. Without trace propagation, retried notifications cannot be correlated to the original order — fix tracing first."},{"scenario":"AuditService is down","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Both OrderService and FulfillmentWorker need resilience for AuditService calls. Audit failures should not block order processing. Consider async audit writes or a local buffer with retry."},{"scenario":"Kafka broker unavailable","handling":"partial","recommendation":"OrderService likely has basic Kafka producer retries, but no mention of fallback behavior. Need a dead-letter strategy and alerting on publish failures."},{"scenario":"Incident investigation across service boundary","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"The broken trace propagation makes cross-service debugging a manual, time-consuming process. This is an operational resilience concern — slow incident response extends outage duration."}],"retry_config":"Not specified for any inter-service HTTP calls. FulfillmentWorker → NotificationService and both callers → AuditService need explicit retry policies with exponential backoff.","timeout_config":"Not specified. All outbound HTTP calls (FulfillmentWorker → NotificationService, OrderService → AuditService, FulfillmentWorker → AuditService) need explicit timeouts to prevent thread/connection pool exhaustion.","circuit_breakers":"None mentioned. At minimum, FulfillmentWorker should have circuit breakers for NotificationService and AuditService to prevent cascading failures.","graceful_degradation":"No evidence of graceful degradation. If AuditService is down, it is unclear whether order processing continues or fails. Audit and notification should be non-blocking to core order flow."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":false,"rollback_plan":"No rollback plan documented. Since trace context propagation requires coordinated changes across FulfillmentWorker, NotificationService, and AuditService, a phased rollout plan with backward compatibility is needed.","monitoring_coverage":"Per-service metrics exist but are insufficient for production operations. No end-to-end SLOs, no Kafka consumer lag monitoring, no cross-service error correlation. An operator cannot answer 'are orders being processed successfully end-to-end?' from current metrics alone.","on_call_documentation":"Effectively unusable for cross-service issues. An on-call engineer cannot follow a request past the Kafka boundary. Runbooks need trace-based investigation procedures, and those require working distributed tracing first.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":["Dead-letter topic for failed Kafka message processing in FulfillmentWorker"],"config_changes":["FulfillmentWorker: Kafka consumer trace ID extraction from message headers","FulfillmentWorker: HTTP client middleware to propagate X-Trace-ID to NotificationService and AuditService","OrderService: HTTP client middleware to propagate X-Trace-ID to AuditService","Timeout and retry configuration for all inter-service HTTP calls"],"infra_provisioning":["Cross-service SLO dashboard","Kafka consumer lag alerting"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database changes indicated in this assessment."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Distributed tracing is broken at the Kafka boundary — 3 of 5 services operate with orphaned traces","reason":"FulfillmentWorker discards the upstream trace ID from Kafka message headers. This single defect severs observability for the entire async processing pipeline. On-call engineers cannot trace requests end-to-end, which directly increases incident duration and mean-time-to-recovery.","routed_to":"Shaq — FulfillmentWorker must be patched to extract and propagate the trace ID from Kafka message headers in all outbound calls"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No cross-service SLO tracking, no Kafka consumer lag monitoring, no dead-letter queue","reason":"Without end-to-end SLOs, the team cannot answer whether the system is healthy. Without consumer lag monitoring, a stalled FulfillmentWorker could silently queue thousands of unprocessed orders. Without a dead-letter queue, poison messages will block processing indefinitely.","routed_to":"Coach K — infrastructure provisioning needed before production readiness can be assessed"}],"confidence":{"level":85,"high_confidence_areas":["Trace ID propagation break at FulfillmentWorker Kafka consumer is a clear, definitive defect","Downstream services (NotificationService, AuditService) are confirmed to lack trace correlation","Per-service metrics exist but cross-service observability is absent"],"low_confidence_areas":["Kafka consumer offset commit strategy and dead-letter handling — not enough detail provided","Specific retry/timeout/circuit breaker implementations — absence noted but internal configs unknown","Whether services have health check endpoints"],"assumptions":["Services use HTTP for synchronous calls and Kafka for async as described","No service mesh or sidecar proxy is handling trace propagation transparently","Structured logging format is consistent at least within each service"]}}
